texto: Step out the front door like a ghostinto the fog where no one notices the contrast of white on white, and in between the moon and you, the angels get a better view of the crumbling difference between wrong and right. I walk in the air, between the rain, through myself and back again where? I don't know.
explicacao: Metáfora atribuída a Counting Crows: compara elementos do cotidiano para falar de limites e escolhas conscientes. O trecho “Step out the front door like a ghostinto the fog where no one notices the contrast of white on wh…” usa imagem concreta para lembrar que reflexao, moon, between pedem movimento e interpretação pessoal, em tom de dia-chuvoso e reflexao.

texto: Well, there's a piece of Maria in every song that I sing. And the price of a memory is the memory of the sorrow it brings. And there is always one last light to turn out and one last bell to ring. And the last one out of the circus has to lock up everything.
explicacao: Citação de Counting Crows sobre temas ligados a reflexao. “Well, there's a piece of Maria in every song that I sing. And the price of a memory is the memory…” articula reflexao, memories, there em registro de reflexao, com relevância para contextos como reflexao.
